name: Koushari (Lentils, Macaroni and Rice in Oil)
recipeInstructions:
This dish is classified as an 'oil' dish by Coptic Egyptians and is prepared during periods of fasting when animal products cannot be consumed. You can cook the lentils, macaroni, and rice in three separate pots or use a single pot as shown below.
1. Place the lentils in a sieve and wash them thoroughly under running water. Transfer to a large pot and add 3 cups of water and 1 teaspoon of salt. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 1 hour until tender but still intact. Drain and set aside.
2. Clean the same pot and add 4 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then add 2 teaspoons of salt and the macaroni. Stir constantly until the water returns to a boil and cook uncovered for 15 minutes until tender. Stir occasionally. Drain and set aside.
3. Clean the pot again and dry it.
4. Wash the rice well in a sieve under running water and drain. Heat oil in the pot and fry the rice over medium heat for 2-3 minutes. Add 2 cups of water and 1 teaspoon of salt, then bring to a boil, stirring occasionally. Cover and simmer over low heat for 15 minutes or until tender. Leave covered off the heat for an additional 5 minutes for the grains to separate.
5. Prepare the ta'leya (see directions below), add tomato puree, and bring to a boil.
6. Add lentils and macaroni to the cooked rice and toss lightly with a fork. Pour hot ta'leya and tomato mixture over the top, toss again, cover the pot, and leave on low heat for 10 minutes. Serve hot.
TA'LEYA:
1. Halve peeled onions lengthwise, then slice thinly to form semicircles.
2. Heat olive oil in a pan, add onions, and fry over medium heat until golden brown. Add garlic and cook for an additional minute.
